# Say Error Handling Test
# Tests error scenarios

# Build the say server binary
go build -o mcp-say-server .

# Test error handling scenarios
exec mcp-say-server <<EOF
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialize","params":{"protocolVersion":"2024-11-05","capabilities":{"roots":{"listChanged":true},"sampling":{}}},"id":1}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"initialized","params":{}}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"say","arguments":{}},"id":3}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"say","arguments":{"text":""}},"id":4}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"say","arguments":{"text":"Test","voice":"NonexistentVoice"}},"id":5}
{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"name":"invalid_tool","arguments":{}},"id":6}
EOF

# Verify missing text error
stdout 'missing required argument.*text'

# Verify empty text handling
stdout 'text cannot be empty'

# Verify invalid voice handling (may succeed or fail depending on system)
# This is platform-dependent behavior

# Verify tool not found error
stdout '"error"'
stdout 'method not found'
